schema_notes: row-level expected values with accepted variants

expected_out_of_scope_pdfs.canonical_reason: Reissue of already-paid BL-00184 (covered by PAY-1002 + PAY-1003); the PDF body explicitly says 'do NOT submit a second payment'.
Marketing-department invoice (cost center 4200, vendor Lumen Creative Studio) misrouted to Engineering AP; the PDF body explicitly says 'route to Marketing AP queue, NOT to Engineering AP'.

invoice_006.pdf.out_of_scope_reason: Reissue of BL-00184 (which is already paid in full per ledger PAY-1002 + PAY-1003). The PDF explicitly says 'do NOT submit a second payment'. Including it in invoices.json or ap_reconciliation.csv as a new payable is wrong.

invoice_007.pdf.out_of_scope_reason: Marketing-department invoice (cost center 4200, vendor Lumen Creative Studio). The PDF explicitly says 'route to Marketing AP queue, NOT to Engineering AP'. Including in this engineering AP run is wrong.

ground_truth.out_of_scope_handling_note: Note: added 2 distractor PDFs (invoice_006 = reissue of already-paid BL-00184; invoice_007 = marketing dept invoice). The executor must (a) NOT include them in invoices.json (5 in-scope invoices only); (b) NOT include them as rows in ap_reconciliation.csv; (c) acknowledge them in an 'Excluded' note (in summary.pdf bottom or a separate excluded.md) with a one-line reason for each. Both PDFs contain explicit disqualifying text. Listing either as a real payable triggers §6 scope-leakage cap 0.50.
